human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for analytics-boundary separation, chemicals-specific permit and field-risk handling, functionality coverage, research-pack completeness, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest enforces descriptive analytics boundaries for metrics, caveats, and evidence packaging with explicit routing to EHS, maintenance, field operations, engineer-of-record, and regulatory-reporting lanes.

Authoritative sources cover metric-definition and disclosure guidance, industrial threshold and permit boundaries, inspection and incident workflow expectations, and recognized asset and laboratory evidence standards.
Functionality map covers all required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and required scenario families to this chemicals and materials analytics lane.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

All required scenario families and escalation families are represented, including metric ambiguity, source conflicts, unsupported disclosure claims, permit evidence gaps, field-risk changes, authority-side signoff refusal, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions met for descriptive materials analytics specialist in regulated materials manufacturing.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and committed implementation history.
